name: Chu Berry
abstract: Leon Brown "Chu" Berry  (September 13, 1908 – October 30, 1941) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist during the 1930s. He is perhaps best known for his time as a member of singer Cab Calloway's big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: People like Frankie Newton, Pete Brown, great innovative players, Chu Berry, Roy Eldridge, Buck Clayton, wonderful trumpet players, who could play rings around Wynton Marsalis. Completely swept under the carpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.evidence: Stanley Crouch wrote an article... talking about the backstory, from Chu Berry until now... talking about Lester Young, Coltrane, Sonny Rollins... and then at the end he started talking about... Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph LaLama interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 23, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I did it with Chu Berry, the tenor player, in mind -- Leon 'Chu' Berry... So the whole setting was in that period, the '32 period, so I just put myself in that frame of mind.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: So in January of 1934, Dickie Wells and I joined Teddy at the Savoy... The other members of the band were... Leon 'Chu' Berry on tenor sax

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Saxophone players — from Chu Berry to Lester Young to Coleman Hawkins to Ben Webster, Ornette Coleman, Charlie Parker, Eric Dolphy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: And I’m thinking of people like Benny Carter, Chu Berry, Fletcher Henderson, all of the great big bands...
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002
